payload.title: Goods Capital Pipeline Starting Point

scope.accountSet: All live Goods-related GHL pipelines, all GHL contacts, and the 14-row investor import set
scope.rankingBasis: QBE-match actionability: signed-commitment likelihood before Aug 31, relationship warmth, stage, amount, blocker count
scope.sourceOfTruth: Live GHL for CRM coverage; investor CSV/wiki pack for capital-raise strategy
scope.sourceScope: GHL contacts, GHL opportunities, investor import CSV, local wiki outputs
scope.actionOwner: Ben/Nic owner model from investor pipeline
scope.ownershipActionModel: Founder-owned next actions; no automated outbound sends
scope.assumptions: Assumption-based: all Goods-related pipelines and investor pack are the candidate universe; buyer/demand records are context unless funder evidence exists.

workNow.whyNow: Proposal / LOI out; shortlist target; AU$200,000 target; first signature unlocks the room before Aug 31.
workNow.primaryContact: Confirm formal grants contact; Sally Grimsley-Ballard is known advisory relationship
workNow.nextStep: Send R4/R5 cover note; ask Snow to be the FIRST signature — decision in next fortnight

workNow.whyNow: In conversation; shortlist target; AU$106,150 target; nearest board decision and Central Australia commitment window.
workNow.primaryContact: Confirm Randle Walker / Jodie for formal board ask
workNow.nextStep: Get next-round beds to a board decision at the ~26 June board; let it count as match-eligible
workNow.sequenceAngle: Forward beds only, Trust-Deed-safe, QBE as multiplier

workNow.whyNow: Contacted; shortlist target; AU$300,000 target; senior debt conversation should start before gates are complete.
workNow.primaryContact: Confirm Hannah Ebeling; Joel Bird may be secondary
workNow.nextStep: Email Hannah Ebeling to open the lender conversation; be honest the 3 gates are unmet
workNow.sequenceAngle: Honest debt-readiness conversation, not a money ask

workNow.whyNow: Proposal / LOI out; shortlist target; AU$640,000 target; EOI is already in; keep warm without chasing.
workNow.primaryContact: Confirm PFI EOI contact before chase
workNow.nextStep: Await EOI feedback; confirm AU$640K still stands and that they will open doors
workNow.sequenceAngle: No-pressure update on EOI and ask for open doors

workNow.whyNow: In conversation; shortlist target; AU$5,000,000 target; later senior anchor; progress in parallel without setting match pace.
workNow.primaryContact: Confirm IBA relationship contact; Nic owns path
workNow.nextStep: Build the business plan to qualify; decide entity/Indigenous-ownership structure
workNow.sequenceAngle: Business plan and ownership structure readiness

suppressOrBlock.evidence: CSV notes: equity-only / legal structure / stage fail.

evidenceGaps: Dedicated QBE-match capital pipeline does not exist in live GHL yet.
The HighLevel connector search did not reliably find Goods capital records; direct LeadConnector API was used for live CRM truth.
Several formal contacts/salutations remain confirm-only: Snow, Centrecorp, SEFA, Tim Fairfax, QBE Foundation, QBE Ventures.
Stage 7 signed/match-eligible total is currently zero in the designed investor pipeline because it has not been imported and no signed commitments are recorded in the pack.
No agreements/e-signature source was checked, so legal commitment status is unverified outside the pack.

candidates.nextAction: Complete PIN working session; submit Stage 2 package — by Sept 2026
candidates.notes: Anchor / program host (this is the match mechanism, not a match-eligible external commitment). Knockout PASS, 3/3 high-fit, 10/10 fit. Stage 1 AU$10K committed; INV-0289 (AU$10,800) outstanding — chase. Stage 2 up to AU$400K, >=1:1 match, legally-binding evidence verified by SIH Sept 2026. Confirm contact: Jay Boolkin (coordinator), Lauren (climate/inclusion).
candidates.contactPath: Confirm Jay Boolkin / Lauren as formal Stage 2 contact
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Send R4/R5 cover note; ask Snow to be the FIRST signature — decision in next fortnight
candidates.notes: SHORTLIST #1 (the unlock). Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. ~AU$493K invoiced since 2023 (accrual, unaudited). Ask AU$130-200K NT-restricted, separate from anchor; co-funds the 50-bed in-source run and anchors the blend. Co-owned Ben+Nic. Confirm contact/salutation: Sally Grimsley-Ballard (advisory group) — may not be the formal grants contact.
candidates.contactPath: Confirm formal grants contact; Sally Grimsley-Ballard is known advisory relationship
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Get next-round beds to a board decision at the ~26 June board; let it count as match-eligible
candidates.notes: SHORTLIST #2 (nearest board decision). Knockout PASS, 3/3, fit 8-10/10. ~130 Stretch Beds (~AU$106,150 incl GST, subject to final quote) to ~26 June board. Trust-Deed-bound to Central Australia — forward-beds only, QBE match named as a multiplier. Prior ~AU$123,332 entangled with voided invoices — DO NOT quote externally. Confirm contact: Randle Walker (relationship), Jodie (on thread).
candidates.contactPath: Confirm Randle Walker / Jodie for formal board ask

crmOpportunityMatches.name: Centrecorp Foundation - 130 Stretch Beds - June 26 board

candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Email Hannah Ebeling to open the lender conversation; be honest the 3 gates are unmet
candidates.notes: SHORTLIST #3 (senior concessional base). Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. ~AU$300K working-capital line at base of the blend; Jigsaw syndicate-agent option. Three gates NOT yet met: Goods-only financial model, independent-majority fiduciary board, serviceable revenue covenant. Near-term conversation, medium-term signature. Confirm contact: Hannah Ebeling (CEO; open by email not LinkedIn); Joel Bird.
candidates.contactPath: Confirm Hannah Ebeling; Joel Bird may be secondary
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Await EOI feedback; confirm AU$640K still stands and that they will open doors
candidates.notes: SHORTLIST #4 (catalytic recoverable layer). Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. AU$640K recoverable-grant target, EOI in — ball in their court, founder cannot accelerate. One soft spot: rated only Somewhat on Connections — confirm they will introduce, not just write a cheque. Jigsaw co-investor.
candidates.contactPath: Confirm PFI EOI contact before chase

crmOpportunityMatches.name: general — paul ramsay foundation — CONTAINED engagement

candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Build the business plan to qualify; decide entity/Indigenous-ownership structure
candidates.notes: SHORTLIST #5 (later senior anchor — NOT first-AU$400K tranche). Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. Up to AU$5M (typical 30% grant / 70% debt); value shown is the facility ceiling, not a near-term ask. Gated on business plan + the entity/Indigenous-ownership decision (which gates eligibility). Owner Nic.
candidates.contactPath: Confirm IBA relationship contact; Nic owns path
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Seek a warm intro via IBA or SIH; research specific deal terms
candidates.notes: BENCH (keep warm). Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. Indigenous-led CDFI; indicative AU$100-500K debt (value = top of band). Position as complementary to / split facility with IBA senior debt. Research-stage — not yet contacted. Owner Nic.
candidates.contactPath: Needs warm intro via IBA or SIH
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Hold warm via Lucy Stronach until the June 2026 board; confirm geography/mandate fit before any ask
candidates.notes: PARKED — knockout UNCONFIRMED (Geography). Killer uncertainty: stated priorities are oceans + modern slavery; fit to remote-housing-durability/economic-inclusion is unconfirmed in the wiki. Every other knockout looks aligned. AU$200K catalytic-bucket target. Gated on June 2026 Minderoo Board justice-strategy decision. Connector: Beck Parkinson. Email lstronach@minderoo.org is real (Lucy Stronach).
candidates.contactPath: Lucy Stronach email is in source; confirm mandate before ask
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Keep warm via Katie Norman; lead with Palm Island + youth jobs; confirm stage/ticket/instrument fit
candidates.notes: PARKED — knockout UNCONFIRMED (Capital Type / Stage / Size). Killer uncertainty: strong geography+mission (QLD, remote, Indigenous, community dev) but profile does not confirm they fund at Goods' early-commercial stage, ticket band, or instrument. Indicative AU$50-150K grant (value = top of band). Grant-layer complement to Snow R4. Confirm contact: Katie Norman.
candidates.contactPath: Confirm Katie Norman as advisory path
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Contact Steph Pearson re a second ask coordinated with the QBE match process
candidates.notes: BENCH / received. Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. Received AU$50K Backing the Future (JOINT with VFFF — one grant, not two); Palm Island acquittal complete Mar 2026. Already a QBE Local Division Partnership — may be receptive to a coordinated second ask. No new ask amount set yet — VALUE BLANK. Contact Steph Pearson (s.pearson@frrr.org.au, real).
candidates.contactPath: Steph Pearson email is in source
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Explore a VFFF Round 2; confirm appetite via N. Chinner / E. Schwanke
candidates.notes: BENCH / received. Knockout PASS, 3/3, 9/10. Co-funder of the AU$50K Backing the Future grant (FRRR administers). Explore Round 2 as part of the philanthropic layer. No new ask amount set yet — VALUE BLANK. Contacts: N. Chinner (nchinner@vfff.org.au), E. Schwanke (elschwanke@vfff.org.au) — both real.
candidates.contactPath: N. Chinner / E. Schwanke emails are in source
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Light-touch — confirm purpose, acquittal, and alumni pathway
candidates.notes: PARKED — knockout UNCONFIRMED (Geography / Size / Stage). Killer uncertainty: thin profile — no stated geographic mandate, ticket range, or stage focus for a follow-on. Received AU$21.9K Spark (Jun 2024); warm. Credentialing relationship, not a core first-AU$400K target. No new ask amount set — VALUE BLANK.
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Return for follow-on or, more valuably, referral to downstream funders
candidates.notes: BENCH / connector. Knockout PASS, 3/3, 10/10. Received AU$130K (Dec 2025); relationship active. Most valuable as an alumni-network CONNECTOR to downstream funders, not a follow-on cheque. No new ask amount set — VALUE BLANK.
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check

candidates.nextAction: Re-evaluate after the legal-structure decision lands (2027); Lighthouse is the only 2026 door
candidates.notes: DECLINED — knockout FAIL. Killer criterion: Capital Type — equity / SAFEs / convertible notes only; Goods needs grants + concessional debt, no equity in 2026. Also fails Legal Structure (equity into eventual CLG/hybrid is complex, forces a new entity) and Stage of Growth (standard cheque AU$5-15M scale-up equity vs early-commercial de-risking). 2/3 high-fit, 7/10. Lighthouse discovery program is the one narrow 2026-compatible pathway. Confirm contact: 'Alex' (first name only in sources).
candidates.source: Investor import CSV + live GHL coverage check
